page: Microsoft Azure D8alds_v6 is an x86_64 architecture virtual machine powered by the AMD EPYC 9V74 processor. Operating under the Microsoft Hyper-V hypervisor, this instance features 8 dedicated vCPUs derived from 4 physical cores with multithreading enabled. The CPU runs at a base clock speed of 3.7 GHz and is equipped with 32 KB L1d cache, 1.0 MB L2 cache, and 32 MB L3 cache. It includes hardware-accelerated instruction sets such as AVX-512, including VNNI and BF16 variants, and cryptographic extensions like AES, VAES, and SHA_NI.

The instance is configured with 16.0 GB of system memory, yielding a low memory-to-vCPU ratio of 2.0 GB per core. This memory constraint represents a clear tradeoff, positioning the server away from memory-intensive workloads. For storage, the server includes a 472 GB local disk and supports Premium Storage. No public IPv4 addresses are bundled with this instance.

Benchmark data indicates strong single-core performance, with stress-ng single-core CPU tests and Geekbench text processing scoring in the top 10% tier. Geekbench single-core scores and small-model LLM text generation with a 135M model rank in the top 25% tier. Conversely, multi-core operations, including stress-ng multi-core and Geekbench multi-core, align with the middle 50% average. Memory bandwidth benchmarks, such as uncached large-block reads at 39.09 GB/sec and writes at 27.76 GB/sec, also fall within average ranges. Medium-model LLM prompt processing with a 7B model represents a performance bottleneck, scoring in the bottom 25% tier.

Given its high single-core compute capabilities paired with a restricted memory profile, this instance offers qualitative cost efficiency for compute-bound tasks that do not require large memory footprints. It is suited for single-threaded processing, text processing, web serving, and lightweight software compilation. It is less suitable for large-scale database deployments or memory-heavy data science pipelines.
description: Microsoft Azure D8alds_v6 is an x86_64 virtual machine featuring 8 dedicated vCPUs and 16.0 GB of RAM, powered by an AMD EPYC 9V74 processor running at 3.7 GHz. Built on the Microsoft Hyper-V hypervisor, it includes a 472 GB local disk and supports Premium Storage. The hardware configuration presents a low memory-to-core ratio of 2.0 GB per vCPU, making it a compute-focused option. Benchmarks show top-tier single-core CPU and text processing performance, alongside strong single-core Geekbench results. However, multi-core performance, memory bandwidth, and database operations remain in the average tier, while medium-model LLM prompt processing is a relative bottleneck. This instance is suited for compute-bound, single-threaded tasks, text processing, and lightweight web serving where memory demands are modest.
og_description: Microsoft Azure D8alds_v6 is an 8-vCPU virtual machine powered by a 3.7 GHz AMD EPYC 9V74 processor. It features 16 GB RAM, a 472 GB local disk, and delivers top-tier single-core CPU performance.
meta_description: Microsoft Azure D8alds_v6 is an 8-vCPU virtual machine with 16 GB RAM and a 472 GB local disk, featuring a 3.7 GHz AMD EPYC processor.
tagline: A compute-focused virtual machine featuring high-frequency AMD EPYC processing, local storage, and exceptional single-core performance for memory-lean workloads.
bullet_points: Powered by an AMD EPYC 9V74 processor running at 3.7 GHz
Equipped with 8 dedicated vCPUs and 16.0 GB of system memory
Includes 472 GB of local storage with Premium Storage support
Top-tier single-core CPU and text processing benchmark results
Best suited for single-threaded tasks, text processing, and web serving
